title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
summary: Google Cloud presented Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration methods that standardize agent connections to data sources. The session covered building agents that access BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB while implementing AgentOps for observability and cost monitoring.
writeup: Google Cloud Tech walks through building agents with the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and connecting them to data sources through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), which standardizes how agents reach systems like BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB. The session pairs construction with AgentOps, covering observability and cost monitoring for agents in operation. The takeaway: MCP provides a common integration layer for data access, and ADK plus AgentOps frames both how agents are assembled and how they are tracked once running.
